Class Game

java.lang.Object
com.cluegame.model.Game
public class Game extends Object
Top-level game controller. Manages turn order, game state and win condition. Handles movement, suggestions, suggestion resolution and accusations.
Author:
Thanh Shaw

  • Constructor Details

    • Game

      public Game(List<Player> players)
      Constructs a new Game with the given list of players.
      Parameters:
      players - list of players (human and/or AI)

    • Game

      public Game(List<Player> players, Scanner scanner)
      Constructs a new Game with the given list of players and a shared Scanner.
      Parameters:
      players - list of players (human and/or AI)
      scanner - the Scanner for reading console input

  • Method Details

    • getStartPositionForToken

      public static int[] getStartPositionForToken(String token)
      Returns the start position for a given token name.
      Parameters:
      token - the short token name (e.g. "Scarlett", "Mustard")
      Returns:
      {row, col} for that character's start, or {0,0} if unknown

    • run

      public void run()
      Runs the full game from start to finish. Sets up the game, then loops through player turns until someone wins or all human players are eliminated. Prints the board at the start of each turn.

    • startGame

      public void startGame()
      Starts the game by shuffling the deck, sealing the murder envelope, dealing remaining cards to players, and placing tokens on start squares.

    • determineFirstPlayer

      private void determineFirstPlayer()
      Sets the first player to whoever holds the Scarlett token. If no player is Scarlett, the first player in the list goes first.

    • printStatus

      private void printStatus()
      Prints the current board state with player positions and a turn header. If multiple humans are playing, shows a handoff screen before the next human's turn so the previous player's information is hidden.

    • nextTurn

      public void nextTurn()
      Executes a single turn for the current player: movement, then suggestion (if in a room), then optional accusation. Advances to the next active player afterward.

    • resolveSuggestion

      private void resolveSuggestion(Player suggester, Suggestion suggestion)
      Resolves a suggestion by going clockwise from the suggesting player. Each other player (including eliminated ones — they still hold cards) checks their hand. The first player with a matching card must show exactly one to the suggester.
      Parameters:
      suggester - the player who made the suggestion
      suggestion - the suggestion to resolve

    • handleAccusation

      private void handleAccusation(Player accuser, Accusation accusation)
      Handles an accusation — checks it against the murder envelope. A correct accusation wins the game. A wrong one eliminates the player.
      Parameters:
      accuser - the player making the accusation
      accusation - the accusation to check

    • moveSuspectToRoom

      private void moveSuspectToRoom(Suggestion suggestion, Room room)
      Moves the accused suspect's playing piece to the suggestion room. Per rule 18, pieces moved by a suggestion stay in their new location. Matches players by checking if the suspect name contains the player's token.
      Parameters:
      suggestion - the suggestion naming the suspect
      room - the room to move the suspect into

    • advanceToNextPlayer

      private void advanceToNextPlayer()
      Advances currentPlayerIndex to the next active player. If no active players remain, ends the game with no winner.

    • allHumansEliminated

      private boolean allHumansEliminated()
      Returns true if all human players have been eliminated (wrong accusation). Used to end the game early when only AI players remain.
      Returns:
      true if no active human players remain

    • printGameOver

      private void printGameOver()
      Prints the game-over message including the winner (if any) and the contents of the murder envelope.

    • clearScreen

      private void clearScreen()
      Prints blank lines to push previous content off the visible terminal. Used for privacy between human players on a shared screen.

    • checkAccusation

      public boolean checkAccusation(Accusation accusation)
      Checks whether an accusation matches the murder envelope.
      Parameters:
      accusation - the accusation being made
      Returns:
      true if accusation is correct

    • getCharacterNames

      public static String[] getCharacterNames()
      Returns the character names for the given player index.
      Returns:
      the full array of classic Clue! character names

    • getTokenNames

      public static String[] getTokenNames()
      Returns the short token names for the given player index.
      Returns:
      the full array of token names

    • getCurrentPlayer

      public Player getCurrentPlayer()

    • getCurrentPlayerIndex

      public int getCurrentPlayerIndex()

    • getPlayers

      public List<Player> getPlayers()

    • isGameOver

      public boolean isGameOver()

    • setGameOver

      public void setGameOver(boolean gameOver)

    • getBoard

      public Board getBoard()

    • getDice

      public Dice getDice()

    • getMurderEnvelope

      public MurderEnvelope getMurderEnvelope()

    • getTurnCount

      public int getTurnCount()

    • getNonPlayerSuspects

      public Map<String,int[]> getNonPlayerSuspects()

    • getWeaponPositions

      public Map<String,String> getWeaponPositions()

    • getWeaponNames

      public static String[] getWeaponNames()

    • moveWeaponToRoom

      public void moveWeaponToRoom(String weaponName, String roomName)
      Moves a weapon token to a room (used during suggestion resolution).
      Parameters:
      weaponName - the weapon to move
      roomName - the room to move it to

    • moveNonPlayerSuspect

      public void moveNonPlayerSuspect(String token, Room room)
      Moves a non-player suspect piece into a room by name. Called when a suggestion names a suspect who is not an active player.
      Parameters:
      token - the suspect's token name
      room - the room to move into
